title: DevcontainerConfig
description: Input contract for bin/gen-devcontainer.sh — describes the stack + knobs that drive a .devcontainer/devcontainer.json emission. Nyann's role here is to pick a stable base image, wire a small set of high-impact VS Code extensions, and embed the right `postCreateCommand` for the package manager. The full devcontainer spec (microsoft/dev-containers) is much wider; this schema covers only the knobs we expose. Operators who need more can hand-edit the emitted file and re-run with --force-overwrite at the next refresh.

language.description: Primary language the devcontainer is built for. Picks the base image, VS Code extension set, and postCreateCommand. Maps 1:1 to StackDescriptor.primary_language with two normalizations: typescript/javascript both → 'node', csharp → 'dotnet'.

name.description: Devcontainer display name. Defaults to the repo basename when omitted; surfaced in the VS Code 'Open in Container' picker.

forwarded_ports.description: Ports to forward from the container to the host. Empty by default — most stacks declare these via a Dockerfile EXPOSE or per-app config and don't need a static list here. Common picks: 3000/4200/5173 for web dev, 8000/8080 for APIs.

post_create_command.description: Override the default per-language postCreateCommand. Useful when the project uses a non-standard package manager (e.g. pnpm rather than npm) or needs additional setup steps (e.g. database migrations). Empty string disables the postCreateCommand entirely.

additional_features.description: Extra `features` entries to merge onto the base set (gh CLI, git-lfs, common-utils). Each entry is the full feature ref including version pin — e.g. 'ghcr.io/devcontainers/features/docker-in-docker:2'. Avoid omitting the version pin; floating tags re-broadcast quietly.

additional_extensions.description: Extra VS Code extension IDs (publisher.extension format) merged onto the per-language base set. Pre-installed in the container.

host_requirements.description: Codespaces machine-size hints. Defaults provide enough headroom for most dev-only workflows; bump for CI-style projects that compile large native dependencies.

snapshot_version.description: Optional. Records the nyann release whose defaults the emitted config snapshots. Surfaced as a comment so a future operator can tell when the file was last regenerated.
